message:
feat(core,pager): WAL-recovery for stale main-file headers, read-only WAL backend install, formatting cleanup

Core — WAL recovery for stale page-1 headers (connection.rs):
- Added install_existing_wal_backend() to PagerBackend that opens a
  pre-existing WAL file in read-only mode to bootstrap the WAL
  backend without requiring a write transaction
- Added install_existing_wal_backend_with_vfs() helper implementing
  the VFS-generic logic: checks WAL existence, opens read-only (with
  READWRITE fallback), verifies minimum WAL header size, then installs
  the WAL backend on the pager
- Added apply_current_journal_mode_to_pager_readonly() on Connection
  that defers to install_existing_wal_backend when set_journal_mode
  returns Unsupported, enabling connections opened in read-only mode
  to still read WAL-mode databases
- Changed wal_file_present_with_vfs to open WAL files READONLY instead
  of READWRITE for probing
- Changed install_wal_backend_with_vfs to try READWRITE first then
  fall back to READONLY for opening existing WAL files
- Changed bootstrap (Connection::open_impl) to use the new readonly
  journal mode path, avoiding Unsupported errors on fresh connections
- Added comprehensive test: test_open_live_c_sqlite_wal_database_with_stale_main_header_page1
  that creates a real C SQLite WAL database via rusqlite with
  autocheckpoint=0, verifies the main-file header is stale (schema_format=0),
  then opens it with frankensqlite and reads back the data successfully

Pager — Stale header recovery (pager.rs):
- Added page_size_from_header_bytes() that parses page size from raw
  header bytes without requiring a full valid DatabaseHeader
- Added stale_main_header_is_wal_recoverable_error() identifying
  header parse errors (InvalidSchemaFormat{raw:0}, InvalidTextEncoding{raw:0})
  that indicate a stale main file under active WAL rather than corruption
- Added stale_main_header_change_counter_under_wal() extracting the
  change counter from a stale header for the WAL refresh comparison
- Added stale_main_header_can_be_recovered_from_live_wal() full check:
  validates the error type, extracts page size, probes for WAL file
  existence and minimum size
- Added bootstrap_header_from_stale_main_file() that constructs a
  synthetic DatabaseHeader from raw bytes with Utf8 encoding placeholder,
  allowing the pager to boot from a stale main file and defer
  authoritative header loading to the WAL snapshot
- Modified PagerInner refresh path to use stale_main_header_change_counter_under_wal
  instead of failing hard when the main-file header is invalid under WAL

Formatting cleanup:
- btree/freelist.rs: Trailing whitespace, reformatted long assert lines
- mvcc/begin_concurrent.rs: Reformatted long concurrent_commit/write_page
  call chains for readability
- mvcc/invariants.rs: Trailing whitespace
- mvcc/materialize.rs: Reformatted from_base_page and
  compute_cell_key_and_sort_key argument lists
- mvcc/seqlock.rs: Reformatted long println! warning
- pager/s3_fifo.rs: Reformatted ghost epoch index insert
- vdbe/vectorized_dispatch.rs: Trailing whitespace

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
